content.problemStatement:
We all know Gru loves Agnes very much. One day Agnes asked Gru to answer some of her queries. She lined up NNN minions in a straight line from 111 to NNN.

You are given an array AAA which contains the height of minions. Agnes will ask him several queries. In each query, Gru has to tell whether the bitwise AND of  A[L…R]A[L \ldots R]A[L…R] is EVEN or ODD. Since Gru is busy planning the biggest heist on Earth, he asks for your help.

###Input:


First line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases.
For each test case:-
First line contains an integer NNN denoting the number of elements.
Second line contains NNN spaced integer representing array elements.
Third line contains QQQ representing number of query.
Next QQQ lines contains two integer LLL and RRR as defined above.
###Output:
For each query, output "EVEN" or "ODD" without quotes.

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
1≤Ai≤1051 \leq A_i \leq 10^51≤Ai​≤105
1≤Q≤1051 \leq Q \leq 10^51≤Q≤105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query, the bitwise AND of 1 and 3 is 1, which is Odd. Hence the first output is ODD.
For the third query, the bitwise AND of 2 and 4 is 0, which is Even. Hence the third output is EVEN.
